name: AYSO Recreational
notes: Recreational; volunteer coaches; Everyone Plays; 50000 teams; 600k+ players 2025; age drop-off: 4U-8U=55% of pool, 10U-12U=28%, 14U=10%, 16U-19U=5%

name: US Youth Soccer Recreational
notes: Largest youth soccer org in US

name: US Club Soccer
notes: Competitive travel; parent org for ECNL

name: ECNL Girls
notes: Premier girls college-prep league

name: ECNL Boys
notes: Boys counterpart; launched 2017

name: MLS NEXT
notes: Top boys tier; 273 clubs 2189 teams 43000+ players (2025-26); TWO TIERS: Homegrown Division (MLS academies+elite) and Academy Division (broader clubs, regionalized); HS play allowed in Academy Div; MLS NEXT Fest 1000+ teams; scholarship initiative 1 fully-funded spot per club per season

name: MLS NEXT Pro Academies
notes: Fully funded by MLS clubs; Homegrown pipeline

name: Girls Academy (GA)
notes: Top girls tier nationally; merit-based 4-tier pathway: GA Open → DPL → ASPIRE → Girls Academy; ASPIRE launched 2025 as bridge tier; moderate-high national travel; strong college recruiting at national events

name: USL Academy
notes: Pre-pro tier; U20 roster model (not age-separated teams); 14yr olds compete vs 16-19yr olds; minimum 6 underage players required per roster; tied to USL Championship/League Two pro franchises; KEY ADVANTAGE: USL Academy Contract allows 14-17yr olds to train/play with pro first team while MAINTAINING NCAA eligibility (unlike MLS Homegrown which forfeits eligibility)

name: USYS National League
notes: Earned entry; regional conferences

name: NPL (National Premier Leagues)
notes: Regional pathway; feeds to ECNL. SUPERSEDED 2026-27: merging with USYS National League into unified N1L platform (see P028). FEE STRUCTURE NOTE: fees set club-by-club with no central schedule; regional operators set competition fees; club annual fees typically $2000-5000 all-in at T2 level; no central published fee list.

name: ODP (Olympic Development Program)
notes: State-run ID program for national teams

name: AYSO EXTRA
notes: Tryout-based selective tier; top 15-20% of local AYSO players; travel across SoCal vs other EXTRA regions; Southern California Extra Development Soccer League (SCEDSL); Everyone Plays rule enforced (50% minimum play time guaranteed); advanced certified volunteer coaches; NO college scout or pro academy presence at matches; ideal springboard to club but not a scholarship/pro pathway

name: USL Youth (Super Y League)
notes: Summer league May-Aug; does NOT conflict with Fall/Spring club team; regional divisions → USL Youth National Finals in Tampa FL (December); SoCal division includes West Coast FC Capo FC Oceanside Breakers; cost ~$575-600 registration + $150 uniform; good national tournament exposure during traditional off-season; complementary to ECNL/MLS NEXT not a replacement

name: National 1 League (N1L)
governing_body: US Club Soccer / US Youth Soccer
notes: NEW 2026-27 season; merger of US Club Soccer NPL and US Youth Soccer National League into single unified platform; ~10000 teams ~150000 players; U13-U19 using Aug 1-Jul 31 age matrix; 8 geographic conferences by population density; top tier of team-based competition below ECNL; integrated postseason with ECNL-RL; promotes to ECNL Regional League; 32 district league operators (see references); affordability-focused; SoCal: SOCAL Soccer League or AZ Soccer Association operates West District 3

name: Development Player League (DPL)
notes: Elite girls tier just below GA; 70+ clubs; regional conferences; HS play allowed U15+; 4-tier pathway: DPL Open→DPL→ASPIRE→GA; age groups shifting Aug 1-Jul 31 in 2026-27

name: GA ASPIRE
notes: NEW 2025; second tier in GA ecosystem; bridge between DPL and full GA; more regional than top GA; collaboration with DPL

name: Elite Academy League (EAL)
notes: Boys tier just below MLS NEXT; on par with or just under ECNL; HS play allowed; EAL and EAL 2 tiers; national showcases; aligned with former USSDA standards

name: National Academy League (NAL)
notes: Launched 2023; just below MLS NEXT/ECNL/GA; often hosts second teams of elite clubs; GotSport registration; 2026-27 expansion to multiple conference regions; college coaches at championships

name: MLS NEXT Homegrown Division
notes: NEW top tier 2025-26; MLS pro academies + elite non-MLS clubs; Homegrown contract pathway; HS play generally not allowed; direct pro pipeline; up to 45 registered players + 9 non-reg prospects per club

name: MLS NEXT Academy Division
notes: NEW second tier 2025-26; broader clubs via NAL/EAL/SDL/Cobalt/Cal North partners; HS play allowed; U13 uses three 25-min periods; every rostered player gets at least one 25-min uninterrupted period

name: Coast Soccer League
notes: SoCal competitive travel league system; tiered divisions D1/D2/D3 with N/W/S sub-divisions; covers LA Inland Empire Orange County San Bernardino; major feeder into ECNL and MLS NEXT; typical club fee ~$3800/yr (real data One Ivy FC 2025); U7-U19; multiple clubs per age group

name: San Diego Surf Junior Academy
notes: Entry-level/developmental tier of Surf organization; U5-U8; three locations (Del Mar/Central/South); 4v4 and 7v7 small-sided; low-pressure technical development; USSF-licensed coaches; no pricing published; separate from competitive ECNL Surf teams (P018 serves U10-U19)

name: Albion SC
notes: MLS NEXT; two locations Ocean Beach and Kearny Mesa San Diego; high touches/practices per week; physical style of play; high ranked teams in most age groups; less emphasis on team tactics vs technical skills; practice fields vary by location; community review: good value with a lot of soccer
